title: Amazon Manaus Boat Rental: Costs, Seasons & Tips for 2026
html:
Exploring the Amazon from Manaus by boat offers an unforgettable journey into the world's largest rainforest. Depart from the Marina do Davi or Porto de Manaus, and expect to pay from €80 per person for a shared tour to €2,500 for a private luxury yacht charter. Visit BoatHire24 to discover your perfect vessel.

Where to set off in Amazon Manaus

The Negro River is the main waterway, and the best departure points include Marina do Davi, a modern facility with fuelling and provisioning. Porto de Manaus, the historic floating harbour, is ideal for day trips. Further upstream, Marina do Careiro offers access to the meeting of the waters. For longer expeditions, consider starting from the town of Iranduba, just across the Negro River bridge, or from Parintins if you plan to visit the island. The iconic Encontro das Águas, where the dark Negro and pale Solimões rivers run side by side, is a must-see destination that lies about 15 nautical miles from Manaus.

Which boat to rent

  1. Speedboat, Best for short day trips to the meeting of the waters, up to 6 guests, from €80 per person.
  2. Small motor yacht (e.g. Azimut 38), Ideal for family exploration of river channels, accommodates 8 guests, from €400 per half-day.
  3. Catamaran, Perfect for sunset cruises and birdwatching, up to 12 guests, from €1,500 per full-day.
  4. Luxury motor yacht (e.g. Maiora 26m), For multi-day wildlife expeditions with crew, sleeps 8 in comfort, from €4,500 per day. You can view the Maiora 26m for hire on BoatHire24.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Amazon Manaus?

Prices range from €80 for a shared tour to €4,500 per day for a crewed luxury yacht.

  • Shared group tours (6, 10 people): from €80 per person, typically 4 hours.
  • Small motorboat half-day (up to 6 guests): from €400.
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 12 guests): from €1,500 including lunch.
  • Crewed luxury yacht per day (8 guests): from €4,500 with crew and fuel.

For accurate, up-to-date pricing and availability, you can compare verified boat listings on the platform, which allows you to see real-time rates from multiple operators in the Manaus region.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Amazon Manaus?

For bareboat rentals you need a valid boating licence, but skippered charters require none. In Brazil, the national authority for boating licences is the Marinha do Brasil. For powerboats over 10m or with engines above 40 hp, a licence is required. Skippered charters include a professional skipper, so no licence is needed. The operator confirms exact requirements based on the vessel. The high season runs from June to November when the water is still high enough to access narrow channels; during the dry season (December to May) the current is slower but some tributaries may be too shallow. The local wind is known as the 'friagem', a cool breeze from the south that can occasionally drop temperatures.

Visit the meeting of the waters early in the morning, before 9am, to see the clearest contrast between the two rivers and avoid the heat of midday.

At a glance

  • Price band: €80, €4,500 per day
  • Peak months: June to November
  • Trip length: 4 hours to 7 days
  • Capacity: 1, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR (local quotes in EUR)

Questions about boat rental in Amazon Manaus

Can I rent a boat for a multi-day trip in the Amazon?

Yes, many operators offer multi-day expeditions, staying overnight on the boat or at jungle lodges, and typically covering 50, 100 nautical miles into the rainforest.

What is the best time of year for boat hire in Manaus?

The high water season from June to November offers the best access to flooded forests and wildlife, while the dry season still provides excellent piranha fishing and pink dolphin sightings.

Is it safe to rent a boat in Amazon Manaus without a guide?

It is not recommended due to navigation hazards and communication constraints; skippered charters are the norm and safer for exploring remote areas.

What marine animals can I see on a boat trip from Manaus?

Expect to see pink and grey river dolphins, caimans, macaws, and possibly monkeys and sloths along the riverbanks, especially in the early morning.

For practical details on making a booking, including cancellation policies and payment options, consult the booking FAQ on BoatHire24 before you confirm your trip.

Whether you are planning a one-day sightseeing cruise or a week-long jungle expedition, renting a boat in Amazon Manaus is straightforward through BoatHire24. If you own a boat in the region, consider using the platform to rent out your boat and connect with travellers from around the world.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Amazon Manaus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
